reasoning: Evidence indicates that Elon Musk was originally appointed as a Special Government Employee or Senior Advisor to lead the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) early in 2025. However, multiple credible sources, including Al Jazeera and Newsweek, report that Musk quit or left the administration in May 2025 and returned to the private sector. A public fallout between Trump and Musk was reported in June 2025, with Musk described as being in the private sector through August 2025. As of the knowledge cutoff on November 9, 2025, there are no official announcements or credible reports of a new nomination or appointment within the specified June 4 to December 31 window. Given the reported falling out and the limited time remaining in the year, the probability of a re-appointment is very low.
